channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office Tuesday, inviting her to answer investigators' questions after she publicly threatened to have the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker assassinated if she were killed
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police in Bangladesh arrest Hindu leader who leads rallies for minority protection
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Police in Bangladesh’s capital have arrested a prominent Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation since former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina fled the country amid a mass uprising in August.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean man convicted for deliberately gaining weight to evade military service
item.description: A South Korean man has been sentenced to a suspended prison term for deliberately gaining weight to evade military service.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Carrefour's cold shoulder for South American beef sparks a backlash from Brazil
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — Supermarket giant Carrefour’s support for French farmers’ protests against a trade agreement between the European Union and the South American bloc Mercosur has sparked a strong reaction in Brazil, including a refusal to supply beef to Carrefour stores in Brazil.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A far-right populist shocks Romania by heading into the presidential runoff
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania is reeling after a little-known, far-right populist secured the first round in the presidential election, beating the incumbent prime minister. Calin Georgescu, who ran independently, will face reformist Elena Lasconi in a Dec. 8.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G7 foreign ministers meet in Italy with hopes of progress toward Mideast ceasefire
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from the world’s leading industrialized nations met Monday as signs emerged of progress on a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah and a certain pressure to advance diplomatic efforts in both the Mideast and Ukraine wars ahead of the new U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of homeowners in England and Wales battle floodwaters after weekend storm
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Hundreds of homeowners in England and Wales were battling floodwaters Monday morning after the second major storm of the winter brought widespread disruption to the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi becomes country's new president
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Uruguay's leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, became the country's new president in a tight runoff Sunday, ousting the conservative governing coalition and making the South American nation the latest to rebuke the incumbent party in a year of landmark elections wor
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea holds memorial for forced laborers in Japan after boycotting Japanese event
item.description: South Korea has paid tribute to wartime Korean forced laborers at Japan’s Sado gold mines in a memorial ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine to analyze fragments of missile fired by Russia capable of carrying nuclear warheads
item.description: Ukrainian investigators allowed journalists to view remains of an intermediate-range ballistic missile used by Russia on the battlefield, before an analysis could be completed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.N. reaches agreement on $300 billion climate deal for developing countries
item.description: The United Nations reached a $300 billion climate agreement for developing countries as they grapple with the significant effects of climate change. Delegates agreed Saturday to the billion-dollar pledge after “weeks of intense negotiations,” the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Arrest warrants for Israeli leaders amplify Democratic rupture
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s (ICC) decision this week to indict Israeli leaders on charges of war crimes in Gaza has reignited the clash among Democrats over Israel's handling of the war with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's former president says US should prioritize helping Ukraine over her country
item.description: Former Taiwanese President Tsai Ing-wen suggested that the U.S. should focus on helping Ukraine before it prioritizes Taiwan in her keynote speech during the annual summit of the Halifax International Security Forum, Politico reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Man in India regains consciousness before his cremation on funeral pyre: reports
item.description: A 25-year-old man who was declared dead in India regained consciousness shortly before he was set to be cremated this week, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rabbi feared kidnapped, killed by terrorists after going missing, prompting investigation
item.description: Rabbi Zvi Kogan, a Chabad emissary, is feared dead after "terrorist involvement" was found related to his disappearance Thursday. Israeli and Emirati officials are investigating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee: Hostages must be released
item.description: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee criticized the U.S. on Friday for pressuring Israel instead of holding Hamas accountable.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC
